reviews.review_description: Don't listen to the people that say it's too hard, it's a batman game what do you expect, batman didn't have powers so it's gonna be hard. It takes me 2 times to beat some of the boss fights but I don't give up, you just gotta be patient, and put it on easy mode if it's too hard. It's a very fun game if you believe in yourself and be patient.

From what I've played the game is amazing and very fluid. I'm a disabled person with limited movement in my right controller because of an amputation, I've never had difficulty playing any vr games, but the combat in this one requires both hands for combat, more than most games, which makes this game impossible for me. I'm putting five stars so the devs might see it better and maybe add an option to turn off specific hands during combat combos.

One thing I didn’t like was how much storage it takes up. I play on the 128 GB Quest 3S and my storage went down way too fast with this game. The overall game is great though, whether it’s swinging from the rafters, throwing punches, or getting beat up, it’s a great story that can show you some events from other Arkham games. The origin of Two-Face, Falcone is in the game, and you get to learn who killed Batman’s parents. It’s an overall great experience and if you have $50 with you I’d recommend getting this.

Haters can say whatever but the game is genuinely fun! It’s also not really recommended for young children! The combat mechanics are cool! They don’t hold your hand but they help you understand the mechanics! If you want easy play it on easy! Normal is for those who want the experience and the difficult fights! The game gets intense so yeah be careful if you’re very sensitive to gun violence, fighting mechanics, etc! Batman didn’t have powers so it’s fun to see the main point of the game perfected!

TLDR INFO AT BOTTOM!

Just finished, and GOSH DANG! This was literally THE BEST VR GAME (on Quest 3) by far. I would closely compare this to Azgard's Wrath II, and this beats it mechanically, immersively, and experience wise.
Now I know they're two different genres of gameplay/combat, but considering both were opening titles to their headsets, I enjoyed this way more, even on the Quest 3!

It's been out for a while, so there's little to no bugs.

Now I'm not a hard-core Batman fan, not knowing much lore, but this game has a hooking story that's easy to follow.

For best experience, I'd recommend playing with immersive setting (or highly immersive, I don't remember) and hard mode, offers a good challenge while still being able to do combos, just gotta be fast. If you want to focus more on combos, go with medium or something.

The only thing I don't like is that it's not long enough. Don't get me wrong, it took me 3 weeks to finish, but if you dedicated your time to this, you can knock it out in 1. Maybe I'm asking for too much, but that's me. Also, I would've loved to see more boss fights. I was a little disappointed I ended with a Predator mission rather than a traditional boss fight. I also wish I had spent more time in Gotham City, but hey, the title is ARKHAM shadow.
And then the price point being $50 is kinda iffy, but I understand people gotta get paid.

TLDR: BEST QUEST VR GAME! Minimal to no bugs, hooking story, easy to learn mechanics/combat, not the longest game, not very much boss fights, but $50 well spent!
Would recommend to new members to VR and veterans.

P.S. I NEED A SEQUEL!

this game is so cool. you actually feel like you are batman instead of just watching him and controlling him. and the storyline is amazing. i know a sequel was in development but it got cancelled because meta wanted to focus on ai glasses but BRING IT BACK PLEASE !!! WE NEED MORE !!!

The second Batman VR game. There's a lot to unpack here and camouflaj did a really fantastic job making a full Batman Arkham experience. These mad lads somehow managed to get the flow combat to well flow good in combat, Stealth is alright but hey it works I guess and the tool placement is great. The batarang being on the chest is a good spot and a really clever reference to the Matt Reeves Batman movie. *SPOILERS STOP READING AND SKIP TO THE END* The Rat King being a two in one villain and showing how Harvey became two-face because of Matches. *SPOILER END* Having the whole game letting Batman be a detective versus a hulking brute most the time is a nice change of pace. Having the ability to be Matches Malone one of the many characters that Bruce uses to get information that Batman can't is SO F***ING COOL. I did receive the product for free with the 3S bundle but it is worth the money. Sucks that you don't have different suits to goof off in but I understand why that isn't the case. The Arkham Shadows suit is a great design honestly and I have a suit mod for Arkham Knight too. If you got the 50 bucks for this game GET IT.

Seriously so much fun. Everyone should play this at least once. Very fun puzzles and excellent gameplay!

I got this game free with my VR headset, and I figured I might as well start with this. Despite being a diehard Marvel fan, I decided that the great Stan Lee couldn’t hold this against me, considering it was free. I had hardly done any VR before, and this game was an unexplainably amazing introduction to it. The story was beautiful, the fights were on-point, and the GRAPHICS. I could go on for hours about the graphics. I enjoy putting family members and friends into the game, merely to see their reactions to it. In fact, it felt so real that the line between the real world and my Batman escapades began to vanish. Before I knew what was happening, I was watching myself become an unstable middle aged man dressed in a bat costume. Every time I played, my voice became an octave deeper, my clothes a shade darker. I suddenly found myself desiring a butler with an accent. It was obvious what was happening, but what could I do to prevent it? If this was truly the fate that I was bound for, then so be it. I knew what was happening, I knew that my ignorance had been my downfall. But I also knew that there was no going back. No returning from this new, dreary reality. Night after night, I found myself standing on top of buildings for hours, finding no cults consisting of rat-people. The occasional petty theft could be glimpsed, but that’s below my pay grade. One night my mother found me, passed out in the front yard after a particularly long night of Batman-ing. She woke me, telling me that I needed help, that I should come inside. And then, it hit me. I wasn’t truly Batman. No, I could never truly be Batman. At least, not until I find a cult. SPECIFICALLY a cult that I could single handedly take down from the inside, much to the concern of my dear butler. Ignoring the cries of my agitated mother, I sprinted off into the awaiting night, finally knowing how to fulfill my duties, knowing that this was my fate, since the moment I was born. I knew what I needed to do, yes, I finally had it! No lunatic would ever actually create a rat cult here, in the real world. I would have to do it myself. But then, of course, there were copyright infringements. No, I could not create a rat cult to call my own. Rather, the new and improved cult should compliment it. It should pay respects to the one that led me here, the one that created who I am today. That’s right. An entire cult dedicated entirely to the reverence of cheese. Therefore respecting the beloved rat-people. From here, I will grow my wonderful cult. Then, and only then, I can take it down from the inside. Just like this accursed game has shown me. I can truly become the almighty Bat once it’s gone up in flames. Nobody will ever suspect me… their leader, a traitor? Don’t make me laugh! It will be beautiful… my revenge will be GLORIOUS. To all the peasants who ever doubted me, your doubting days are done. I will be revered worldwide. Everyone will know my name. Oh, but that’s not it. Ohhh no. Once the whole world bows down to me, and only to me, ONLY THEN WILL I-

Whoops, how strange. That wind sure is crazy today, who the heck said all of that.. right guys? Anyways, great game, the details and story are amazing. Highly recommend!

Easily my second favorite Arkham game. Nearly everything you could do in the console games is here and accounted for. While not open world like the other Arkham games, it makes up for it by being similar to Asylum, yet having all the things that city added to make it better overall.

If you're a fan of the Rocksteady Studios Batman games you'll like this game, it is very difficult to describe but it literally feels like you're playing one of those but in VR. For me personally it's the combat system that sells it, every time I get hit it feels like it's my own fault and it's a fair challenge

The hand to hand combat is magnificent I
It’s maybe the most detailed virtual reality game I’ve ever played I just wish there was a mode where you are able to explore the city and stop hostages and missions like that on your accords . But otherwise it’s a beautiful game

reviews.review_title: Game is a Waste - Can’t beat Bolton
reviews.review_description: Yes, I’m a senior, but I’ve been playing video games for 30 years and never before had to quit before the end. The Bolton battle is impossible for me. I’ve researched the web, tried 100 times, and still can’t proceed and there appears to be no alternative but quit. I’m so extremely frustrated, and will never buy from this producer again. There should be some mechanism to bypass or ease the difficulty if you’re going to insert a battle that is much more difficult than anything else in the game. I got past all the difficult battles leading up to Bolton, and so disappointed I can’t finished the game.

This was an amazing game and I was definitely lucky to get this game for free along with buying my vr. After downloading this game with nothing else to play on vr I was astonished by how well built this game was. The graphics are beautiful and beyond any of my expectations for a 3s. After getting addicted to this game I finished it in under a month. Now I’m sitting here asking the devs to add more to this game. If you have the money for this game just know that it is definitely worth it and has a lot of fun to offer you. But for how much money this game is it has an extremely short storyline. I can’t be one to talk as I got this game for free but I would still enjoy more content.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games in VR

Great game play and graphics. Writing is good but should have been better. Good plot twists with emotional impact. But there's some annoying woke-pandering to interrupt the fun. And the game is more linear than it should be. At the end, you face a tough moral choice, but you don't get to make it. You just follow player-instructions and see what choice is made by your character. 
Oh, and there's that wedged-in scene with the Joker that adds nothing but plot holes.
It almost feels like they had a much better game and then went out of their way to insert flaws.

Everything in the game feels very responsive, the physics, the combat, everything. If you've played the Arkham Trilogy on flat-screen, then you'll love this game. It was worth the $50, hell maybe even $60.

reviews.review_title: Just get b&s if you want combat
reviews.review_description:
I went into this for the combat, but I was disappointed as I feel that this game could be played on any device that can run it. The controls are just bad, and the combat is janky at best. I do not recommend this to anyone who is not only here for the story or is really into batman (or has another reason to not be able to play other games).

Basically, to sum it all up, if you want a superhero sim, then play battleglide, and if you want superb combat, get bonelab or blade and sorcery nomad.

I do not have anything against this game. I just do not feel that it was made for vr.

I did have issues with running previous chapters from the bat cave and having some guidance for the task would be nice the 3d map used it's not the best for navigation.

When I finally got my quest 3 this was the first game I bought and Jesus Christ it's amazing once you play it yourself.

reviews.review_title: A Workout Disguised as a Game. LOVE IT!
reviews.review_description:
Man… I LOVE this game. Easy 5 stars.

Batman: Arkham Shadow isn’t just fun, it’s a mostly full-on physical workout. There’s so much movement, dodging, punching, and action that I actually wait until after my real workout to play. That’s how intense it can be. I love stepping into Batman’s body.

The combat is incredible, the immersion is top-tier, and the VR mechanics make you feel like you’re actually in the environment. Throwing punches, blocking, moving through fights. It’s exhausting in the best possible way.

I’m about 75% through the story and already sad it’s going to end. That’s always the sign of a great game that you don’t want to be over.

What really frustrates me is knowing the studio got shut down because this absolutely deserves a sequel. There’s so much potential here and it’s a shame we might not see more.

Still, as it stands, this is one of the best VR experiences I’ve ever played next to Asgard’s Wrath. If you own a headset, this is a must-play.

amazing as a fan of Batman this is amazing 
🦇🙋🏻‍♂️

When the game was announced, I had the same reaction as the rest of the fanbase, I was sad to see that the game was a Meta Quest exclusive. Fortunately, thanks to BatmanArkhamVideos, I decided to make the jump and buy a Meta Quest 3s, in order to be able to play the game. I am nothing short of thrilled with my experience so far, and I can’t wait to jump back into the game shortly.

Good game btw but i kinda cried at the end but i gotta play the xbox version but also clif hangar sad that it ended w game

This game is one of the most fun games I've ever played on quest. Its one of those big budget meta funded AAA games and im all for it. Too bad meta shut down camouflage. 

The gameplay is fast and engaging with lots of enemy variety and takedown ways. The stealth sections are fun. The bosses are also really fun aswell.

The graphics on this game are on another level. This is the best looking quest game on the platform maybe with the exception of asgards wrath 2. The lighting and shadow are amazing. You 
All in all your feel like batman.

Now the story is my main issue. I want to say that I love it but I dont. You will know who the rat king was from the start of the game or maybe thats just me. The cutscenes are fine but nothing special. You also dont spend enough time with the person who is revealed to be the rat king enough to care unless you have played other batman games before this. I do like black gate being used as the main bulk of the game however I wouldn't have minded more time in gotham. Lastly there is some pacing issues but its not that big of a deals.

All in all the most 8/10 batman games I've ever played.

This is a great game the combat and weapon systems are really good. It feels like The Arkham knight game which is also good. The combat of following a guide is done perfectly and feels great. The puzzles aren’t that difficult but will have you sitting there for a bit (or maybe I’m not that smart IDK) the story is also pretty good. The game is very polished and the graphics are pretty good. Basically a good Batman game. :)

An astonishingly great translation of the Arkham formula into VR. Gameplay wise in my opinion, Arkham Shadow is perfect.

From a story standpoint, the game is more flawed and I’m sure some will find the sections where you aren’t Batman less favorable. But those issues are minor at best. 

If you don’t like the Arkham games, Arkham Shadow won’t change your mind. But if you do or just like action narrative games, it’s a must play.

A sequel would be an instant buy, if one ever arrives given that Meta gutted the studio.

I have this account on my VR, then I get a message that I needed to create a separate account for my son, once I did that, my original account vanished, I tried to sign in and it took me to my son's account, how do I recover my account back so I can play this game.

This doesn’t come close to asguards wrath 2, the fighting is scripted, graphics are mediocre and the story is predictable. Really hate games that cheat to get an advantage, its childish and limited. 4/10

reviews.review_title: The game is fun but combat isn’t
reviews.review_description: The game feels really solid, the story is good, interacting with things feels nice, the graphics are great for a quest game, but I don’t like the way they made the combat. It feels like combat that would have been better with a controller, if I have 6DOF for my arms, then I would want to be able to punch where I want instead of having prompts where I have to use the same hand twice instead of being able to alternate, the lunging at enemies is also a little disorienting as well. If the combat was less scripted and more open, then my review would definitely be 5 stars.
